task: I’m putting together a short intro for a project handoff and need it to cover the essentials without dragging on. What’s a straightforward way to open it so the next person has the key details right away?

turns.agent_response:
Open with: **what the job is, where it is, what’s done, what’s left, and what needs attention next.**

A clean handoff intro:

**“This is [project name/location]. We’ve completed [finished work], and the next step is [next task]. Watch [key issue/constraint], and don’t move forward until [required sign-off/condition] is cleared.”**

If you want, I can turn that into a **3-sentence handoff script** or a **clipboard-style template**.

user_simulator.text: Close, but make it tighter. I want something I can say in one breath at the start of a handoff. Give me a version that sounds like a site supervisor, not a template. Just the opener and maybe one backup line if the next guy needs the warning right away.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a tighter site-supervisor opener:

**“Here’s the handoff: job’s at [location], [done work] is in, [next step] is next, and the hold-up is [issue]. Don’t push past [sign-off/condition] until it’s cleared.”**

Backup warning line:

**“If you hit [issue], stop and get it checked before you move.”**

If you want, I can make it even more like **truck talk** or turn it into a **10-second version**.

user_simulator.text: Better. Still a little stiff though. I want it to sound like I’m actually standing there, not reading off a card. Make it harsher, more like: job, what’s done, what’s next, what can’t slip. And keep the warning line short — something I can throw out without thinking.

turns.agent_response:
**“Job’s at [location]. [Done work] is in, [next step] is up, and [issue] can’t slip.”**

Backup line:

**“If [issue] shows up, stop and get it checked.”**

user_simulator.text:
Too clean. Needs more bite.

Try:
**“Job’s [location]. [Done work] is in, [next step] is next, and [issue] doesn’t slip.”**

Backup:
**“See [issue], stop.”**

turns.agent_response:
**“Job’s [location]. [Done work] is in, [next step] is next, and [issue] doesn’t slip.”**

Backup:

**“See [issue], stop.”**

user_simulator.text:
Closer. Still a little neat. I’d want it rougher, more like: **“Job’s [location]. [done work] is in, [next step] is up, and [issue] is the one that can’t slip.”**

Backup: **“See [issue], stop cold.”**

turns.agent_response:
**“Job’s [location]. [done work] is in, [next step] is up, and [issue] is the one that can’t slip.”**

Backup:

**“See [issue], stop cold.”**

user_simulator.text:
Yeah. That’s it. Tight, rough enough, no fluff.

**“Job’s [location]. [done work] is in, [next step] is up, and [issue] is the one that can’t slip.”**

**“See [issue], stop cold.”**
